quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure, e.g. West is "roughly 65% of North's total" and Gadget is "33.43% of revenue" rather than bare rankings.

specificity.why: Each recommendation names a product/region with data, e.g. "Reassess Sprocket pricing: high volume at $4.75 yields just 15.80% of revenue."

insight_density.why: Surfaces multiple non-obvious, actionable observations such as mean-above-median skew, Gadget's price-driven lead "despite modest unit volume," and Sprocket's high-volume/low-revenue mismatch.

quantification.why: Essentially every comparison carries a computed figure — e.g. Gadget "$2,392.00 (33.43% of revenue)" and West "roughly 65% of North's total" and "trails leading North by 35%".

specificity.why: Each recommendation names a product/region with data, e.g. "Reassess Sprocket pricing: high volume at $4.75 yields just 15.80% of revenue" and "Grow West region sales: at $1,414.82 it trails leading North by 35%".

insight_density.why: Multiple non-obvious, actionable observations appear, such as the mean/median skew ("a few high-value orders pull the average above the midpoint") and Sprocket's high-volume-low-revenue mismatch at "$4.75".

quantification.why: Nearly every comparison carries a computed figure — percentages ('33.43% of revenue'), ratios ('roughly 65% of North's total'), and gaps ('trails leading North by 35%').

specificity.why: Each recommendation names a specific product/region with figures and an action ('Reassess Sprocket pricing: high volume at $4.75 yields just 15.80% of revenue').

insight_density.why: Surfaces multiple non-obvious observations — the median/mean skew ('a few high-value orders pull the average above the midpoint'), Gadget's price-driven lead 'despite modest unit volume', and Sprocket's high-volume/low-revenue mismatch.
